1.iwh_en: It was narrated from Abu Umamah (may Allah be pleased with him) that the Messenger of Allah ﷺ said: 'Jibril (peace be upon him) never came to me except that he commanded me to use the siwak. I feared that I would wear down the front of my teeth.'
1.iwh_id: Diriwayatkan dari Abu Umamah (radhiyallahu 'anhu) bahwa Rasulullah ﷺ bersabda: 'Jibril (alaihissalam) tidak pernah datang kepadaku kecuali ia memerintahkanku untuk bersiwak. Sungguh aku khawatir akan mengikis bagian depan giginya.'

2.iwh_en: It was narrated from Abu Umamah (may Allah be pleased with him) that the Messenger of Allah ﷺ said: 'Love (al-miqah) comes from Allah and is cast down from the heavens. When Allah loves a servant, He says to Jibril: I love so-and-so, so love him. And Jibril calls out: Indeed Allah loves so-and-so, so love him. And love for him is placed in the earth. And when Allah hates a servant, He says to Jibril: I hate so-and-so, so hate him. And Jibril calls out: Indeed your Lord hates so-and-so, so hate him. And hatred for him spreads through the earth.'
2.iwh_id: Diriwayatkan dari Abu Umamah (radhiyallahu 'anhu) bahwa Rasulullah ﷺ bersabda: 'Kecintaan (al-miqah) berasal dari Allah dan diturunkan dari langit. Apabila Allah mencintai seorang hamba, Dia berfirman kepada Jibril: Aku mencintai si fulan, maka cintailah dia. Lalu Jibril berseru: Sesungguhnya Allah mencintai si fulan, maka cintailah dia. Dan kecintaan padanya pun tertanam di bumi. Dan apabila Allah membenci seorang hamba, Dia berfirman kepada Jibril: Aku membenci si fulan, maka bencilah dia. Lalu Jibril berseru: Sesungguhnya Rabbmu membenci si fulan, maka bencilah dia. Dan kebencian padanya pun menyebar di bumi.'

3.iwh_en: It was narrated from Abu Umamah (may Allah be pleased with him) that the Messenger of Allah ﷺ said: 'Whoever performs wudu properly — washing his hands and face and wiping over his head and ears — then stands for the obligatory prayer, Allah will forgive him on that day whatever his feet walked toward, whatever his hands seized, whatever his ears heard, whatever his eyes saw, and whatever evil he entertained in his heart.' Abu Umamah said: 'By Allah, I heard it from the Prophet of Allah ﷺ more times than I can count.'
3.iwh_id: Diriwayatkan dari Abu Umamah (radhiyallahu 'anhu) bahwa Rasulullah ﷺ bersabda: 'Barangsiapa berwudu dengan sempurna — membasuh kedua tangan dan wajahnya serta mengusap kepala dan kedua telinganya — kemudian mendirikan shalat fardhu, maka Allah akan mengampuninya pada hari itu atas apa yang dilakukan kakinya, yang digenggam tangannya, yang didengar telinganya, yang dilihat matanya, dan keburukan yang terlintas dalam hatinya.' Abu Umamah berkata: 'Demi Allah, aku mendengarnya dari Nabi Allah ﷺ berkali-kali hingga tak terhitung.'

4.iwh_en: It was narrated from Abu Umamah (may Allah be pleased with him) that the Messenger of Allah ﷺ said: 'A prayer performed right after another prayer with no idle talk between them is written in Illiyyun.'
4.iwh_id: Diriwayatkan dari Abu Umamah (radhiyallahu 'anhu) bahwa Rasulullah ﷺ bersabda: 'Shalat yang dikerjakan tepat setelah shalat lainnya tanpa diselingi perkataan sia-sia di antara keduanya, dicatat di Illiyyun.'

5.iwh_en: It was narrated from Abu Umamah (may Allah be pleased with him) that the Prophet ﷺ said: 'Fever is a bellows from the Hellfire. Whatever of it afflicts the believer is his share of the Hellfire.'
5.iwh_id: Diriwayatkan dari Abu Umamah (radhiyallahu 'anhu) bahwa Nabi ﷺ bersabda: 'Demam adalah hembusan dari neraka Jahannam. Apa yang menimpa seorang mukmin darinya adalah bagiannya dari neraka Jahannam.'

6.iwh_en: It was narrated from Abu Umamah (may Allah be pleased with him) that the Prophet ﷺ said: 'When a man performs wudu as he has been commanded, sin departs from his hearing, his sight, his hands, and his feet.' Abu Umamah said he would not have narrated it unless he had heard it from the Prophet ﷺ at least seven times.
6.iwh_id: Diriwayatkan dari Abu Umamah (radhiyallahu 'anhu) bahwa Nabi ﷺ bersabda: 'Apabila seseorang berwudu sebagaimana yang diperintahkan, dosa akan pergi dari pendengarannya, penglihatannya, kedua tangannya, dan kedua kakinya.' Abu Umamah berkata bahwa ia tidak akan menceritakannya kecuali karena telah mendengarnya dari Nabi ﷺ setidaknya tujuh kali.'

7.iwh_en: It was narrated from Abu Umamah (may Allah be pleased with him) that a man said: 'O Messenger of Allah, tell me of a deed that will enter me into Paradise.' He ﷺ said: 'Hold fast to fasting, for there is nothing equal to it — or nothing like it.'
7.iwh_id: Diriwayatkan dari Abu Umamah (radhiyallahu 'anhu) bahwa seorang laki-laki berkata: 'Wahai Rasulullah, beritahukan kepadaku amal yang akan memasukkanku ke surga.' Beliau ﷺ bersabda: 'Hendaklah kamu berpuasa, karena tidak ada yang setara dengannya — atau tidak ada yang semisal dengannya.'

8.iwh_en: It was narrated from Abu Umamah (may Allah be pleased with him) that the Messenger of Allah ﷺ said: 'Blessed is the one who has seen me. And seven times blessed is the one who believed in me without seeing me.'
8.iwh_id: Diriwayatkan dari Abu Umamah (radhiyallahu 'anhu) bahwa Rasulullah ﷺ bersabda: 'Berbahagialah orang yang telah melihatku. Dan tujuh kali kebahagiaan bagi orang yang beriman kepadaku tanpa pernah melihatku.'

9.iwh_en: It was narrated from Abu Umamah (may Allah be pleased with him) that the Prophet ﷺ said: 'There is no Muslim man who looks at the beauty of a woman for the first time and then lowers his gaze, except that Allah creates for him an act of worship whose sweetness he will find.'
9.iwh_id: Diriwayatkan dari Abu Umamah (radhiyallahu 'anhu) bahwa Nabi ﷺ bersabda: 'Tidaklah seorang muslim yang memandang kecantikan seorang wanita pertama kali kemudian menundukkan pandangannya, melainkan Allah akan menciptakan baginya suatu ibadah yang akan ia rasakan kemanisannya.'

10.iwh_en: It was narrated from Abu Umamah (may Allah be pleased with him), attributing it to the Prophet ﷺ, that he said: 'Whoever initiates the greeting of peace is closer to Allah, the Mighty and Majestic, and to His Messenger ﷺ.'
10.iwh_id: Diriwayatkan dari Abu Umamah (radhiyallahu 'anhu), yang menyandarkannya kepada Nabi ﷺ, bahwa beliau bersabda: 'Barangsiapa memulai salam, maka dialah yang lebih dekat kepada Allah Yang Maha Perkasa lagi Maha Agung dan kepada Rasul-Nya ﷺ.'

11.iwh_en: It was narrated from Abu Umamah (may Allah be pleased with him) that the Messenger of Allah ﷺ said: 'Do not sell female singers, nor buy them, nor teach them, and there is no good in trading them. Their price is forbidden.'
11.iwh_id: Diriwayatkan dari Abu Umamah (radhiyallahu 'anhu) bahwa Rasulullah ﷺ bersabda: 'Janganlah kalian menjual penyanyi wanita, jangan membelinya, jangan mengajarinya, dan tidak ada kebaikan dalam memperdagangkannya. Harganya adalah haram.'

12.iwh_en: It was narrated from Abu Umamah (may Allah be pleased with him) that the Prophet ﷺ said: 'When a man performs wudu as he has been commanded, sin departs from his hearing, his sight, his hands, and his feet.' Abu Umamah said he would not have narrated it unless he had heard it from the Prophet ﷺ at least seven times.
12.iwh_id: Diriwayatkan dari Abu Umamah (radhiyallahu 'anhu) bahwa Nabi ﷺ bersabda: 'Apabila seseorang berwudu sebagaimana yang diperintahkan, dosa akan pergi dari pendengarannya, penglihatannya, kedua tangannya, dan kedua kakinya.' Abu Umamah berkata bahwa ia tidak akan menceritakannya kecuali karena telah mendengarnya dari Nabi ﷺ setidaknya tujuh kali.'

13.iwh_en: It was narrated from Abu Umamah (may Allah be pleased with him) that the Prophet ﷺ performed wudu and washed his face three times, his hands three times each, and wiped over his head. He said: 'The ears are part of the head.' Hammad said: I do not know whether this was the statement of Abu Umamah or of the Prophet ﷺ. And the Messenger of Allah ﷺ used to wipe over his leather socks.
13.iwh_id: Diriwayatkan dari Abu Umamah (radhiyallahu 'anhu) bahwa Nabi ﷺ berwudu dan membasuh wajahnya tiga kali, kedua tangannya masing-masing tiga kali, dan mengusap kepalanya. Beliau bersabda: 'Kedua telinga adalah bagian dari kepala.' Hammad berkata: Aku tidak tahu apakah ini perkataan Abu Umamah atau perkataan Nabi ﷺ. Dan Rasulullah ﷺ biasa mengusap atas dua khuf-nya.'

14.iwh_en: It was narrated from Abu Umamah (may Allah be pleased with him) that the Prophet ﷺ said: 'Differ from the People of the Book.' He was told: 'O Messenger of Allah, the People of the Book wear sandals without socks.' He ﷺ said: 'Then wear sandals and socks, and differ from the People of the Book.' He was told: 'The People of the Book trim their mustaches and let their beards grow.' He ﷺ said: 'Trim your mustaches and let your beards grow, and differ from the People of the Book.'
14.iwh_id: Diriwayatkan dari Abu Umamah (radhiyallahu 'anhu) bahwa Nabi ﷺ bersabda: 'Berbedalah dari Ahli Kitab.' Dikatakan kepadanya: 'Wahai Rasulullah, Ahli Kitab memakai sandal tanpa kaus kaki.' Beliau ﷺ bersabda: 'Maka pakailah sandal dan kaus kaki, dan berbedalah dari Ahli Kitab.' Dikatakan: 'Ahli Kitab memangkas kumis mereka dan membiarkan janggut mereka panjang.' Beliau ﷺ bersabda: 'Cukurlah kumis kalian dan biarkanlah janggut kalian panjang, dan berbedalah dari Ahli Kitab.'

15.iwh_en: It was narrated from Abu Umamah (may Allah be pleased with him) that the Prophet ﷺ said: 'Whoever strokes the head of an orphan boy or girl, doing so only for the sake of Allah, will have for every hair that his hand passes over good deeds. And whoever treats well an orphan girl or boy in his care, I and he will be in Paradise like these two' — and he joined his two fingers together.
15.iwh_id: Diriwayatkan dari Abu Umamah (radhiyallahu 'anhu) bahwa Nabi ﷺ bersabda: 'Barangsiapa mengusap kepala anak yatim laki-laki atau perempuan, tidak melakukannya kecuali karena Allah, maka baginya untuk setiap helai rambut yang dilewati tangannya adalah kebaikan. Dan barangsiapa berbuat baik kepada anak yatim perempuan atau laki-laki yang ada di sampingnya, maka aku dan dia di surga seperti dua ini' — beliau merapatkan dua jarinya.'

16.iwh_en: It was narrated from Abu Umamah (may Allah be pleased with him) regarding the verse: 'He will drink from festering water, sipping it but hardly swallowing it' — the Prophet ﷺ said: 'It will be brought close to him and he will recoil from it. When it comes near his face, it will scorch it and the scalp of his head will fall off. When he drinks it, it will cut through his intestines until it exits from his rear. Allah says: And they will be given boiling water to drink, which will cut apart their intestines. And He says: If they call for relief, they will be relieved with water like molten metal that scalds the faces — how dreadful a drink.'
16.iwh_id: Diriwayatkan dari Abu Umamah (radhiyallahu 'anhu) mengenai ayat: 'Dan ia akan diminumkan air nanah, yang ia teguk namun hampir tidak bisa menelannya' — Nabi ﷺ bersabda: 'Air itu akan didekatkan kepadanya dan ia akan merasa jijik. Ketika sudah dekat dengan wajahnya, air itu akan membakar wajahnya dan kulit kepalanya akan mengelupas. Ketika ia meminumnya, air itu akan memotong ususnya hingga keluar dari duburnya. Allah berfirman: Dan mereka akan diberi minum air mendidih yang memotong usus-usus mereka. Dan Dia berfirman: Jika mereka meminta pertolongan, mereka akan ditolong dengan air seperti logam cair yang membakar wajah — seburuk-buruk minuman.'

17.iwh_en: It was narrated from Abu Umamah (may Allah be pleased with him) that a man came to the Messenger of Allah ﷺ and said: 'O Messenger of Allah, I have committed a sin worthy of a prescribed punishment, so carry it out on me.' The Prophet ﷺ turned away from him. The prayer was established, and when the Prophet ﷺ finished with the salam, the man stood and said: 'O Messenger of Allah, I have committed a sin deserving a prescribed punishment, so carry it out on me.' He ﷺ said: 'Did you perform wudu when you came?' He said: 'Yes.' He said: 'Did you pray with us when we prayed?' He said: 'Yes.' He said: 'Go, for indeed Allah has forgiven you.'
17.iwh_id: Diriwayatkan dari Abu Umamah (radhiyallahu 'anhu) bahwa seorang laki-laki datang kepada Rasulullah ﷺ dan berkata: 'Wahai Rasulullah, aku telah melakukan dosa yang layak mendapat hukuman, maka tegakkanlah hukuman itu atasku.' Nabi ﷺ berpaling darinya. Shalat pun ditegakkan, dan ketika Nabi ﷺ selesai dengan salam, laki-laki itu berdiri dan berkata: 'Wahai Rasulullah, aku telah melakukan dosa yang layak mendapat hukuman, maka tegakkanlah hukuman itu atasku.' Beliau ﷺ bersabda: 'Apakah kamu berwudu ketika kamu datang?' Dia berkata: 'Ya.' Beliau bersabda: 'Apakah kamu shalat bersama kami ketika kami shalat?' Dia berkata: 'Ya.' Beliau bersabda: 'Pergilah, karena sesungguhnya Allah telah mengampunimu.'

18.iwh_en: It was narrated from Abu Umamah (may Allah be pleased with him) that while the Messenger of Allah ﷺ was walking in intense heat, the strap of his sandal broke. A man came to him with a strap and placed it in his sandal. The Messenger of Allah ﷺ said: 'If you knew what you have burdened the Messenger of Allah ﷺ with, you would not have placed that burden on the Messenger of Allah ﷺ.'
18.iwh_id: Diriwayatkan dari Abu Umamah (radhiyallahu 'anhu) bahwa ketika Rasulullah ﷺ sedang berjalan dalam terik yang hebat, tali sandalnya putus. Seorang laki-laki datang membawa tali dan memasangnya pada sandal beliau. Rasulullah ﷺ bersabda: 'Seandainya kamu mengetahui beban yang kamu tanggungkan kepada Rasulullah ﷺ, niscaya kamu tidak akan menanggungkan beban itu kepada Rasulullah ﷺ.'

19.iwh_en: It was narrated from Abu Umamah (may Allah be pleased with him) that he asked the Messenger of Allah ﷺ many questions, including: 'O Prophet of Allah, what is charity?' He ﷺ said: 'Manifold multiples, and with Allah there is even more.' He asked: 'O Prophet of Allah, which charity is best?' He ﷺ said: 'Giving in secret to the poor, and striving from one who has little.' He asked: 'O Prophet of Allah, which revelation sent to you is greatest?' He ﷺ said: 'Allah — there is no god but He, the Ever-Living, the Sustainer of all — the Verse of the Throne.' He asked: 'O Prophet of Allah, which martyr is best?' He ﷺ said: 'One whose blood is shed and whose horse is hamstrung.' He asked: 'O Prophet of Allah, which slaves to free are best?' He ﷺ said: 'The most expensive and the most precious to their owners.' He asked: 'O Prophet of Allah, which prophet came first?' He ﷺ said: 'Adam.' He asked: 'O Prophet of Allah, was Adam a prophet?' He ﷺ said: 'Yes, a prophet spoken to directly, whom Allah created with His Hand, then blew into him his spirit, then said to him: O Adam, face Me.' He asked: 'O Messenger of Allah, how many prophets were there in total?' He ﷺ said: 'One hundred and twenty-four thousand, of whom the messengers numbered three hundred and fifteen — a vast multitude.'
19.iwh_id: Diriwayatkan dari Abu Umamah (radhiyallahu 'anhu) bahwa ia bertanya kepada Rasulullah ﷺ banyak pertanyaan, di antaranya: 'Wahai Nabi Allah, apakah sedekah itu?' Beliau ﷺ bersabda: 'Berlipat ganda, dan di sisi Allah masih ada yang lebih banyak lagi.' Ia bertanya: 'Wahai Nabi Allah, sedekah manakah yang paling utama?' Beliau ﷺ bersabda: 'Memberi secara diam-diam kepada orang fakir, dan bersungguh-sungguh dari orang yang memiliki sedikit.' Ia bertanya: 'Wahai Nabi Allah, wahyu manakah yang diturunkan kepadamu yang paling agung?' Beliau ﷺ bersabda: 'Allah — tidak ada tuhan selain Dia, Yang Maha Hidup, Yang terus-menerus mengurus makhluk-Nya — Ayat Kursi.' Ia bertanya: 'Wahai Nabi Allah, syahid manakah yang paling utama?' Beliau ﷺ bersabda: 'Yang darahnya ditumpahkan dan kudanya dibunuh.' Ia bertanya: 'Wahai Nabi Allah, membebaskan budak manakah yang paling utama?' Beliau ﷺ bersabda: 'Yang paling mahal harganya dan paling berharga bagi pemiliknya.' Ia bertanya: 'Wahai Nabi Allah, nabi manakah yang pertama?' Beliau ﷺ bersabda: 'Adam.' Ia bertanya: 'Wahai Nabi Allah, apakah Adam seorang nabi?' Beliau ﷺ bersabda: 'Ya, seorang nabi yang diajak berbicara langsung, yang Allah ciptakan dengan Tangan-Nya, kemudian meniupkan ruh ke dalamnya, kemudian berfirman kepadanya: Wahai Adam, menghadaplah kepada-Ku.' Ia bertanya: 'Wahai Rasulullah, berapa jumlah total para nabi?' Beliau ﷺ bersabda: 'Seratus dua puluh empat ribu, di antaranya para rasul berjumlah tiga ratus lima belas — jumlah yang sangat banyak.'

20.iwh_en: It was narrated from Abu Umamah (may Allah be pleased with him) that the Messenger of Allah ﷺ passed by a man reciting 'Say: He is Allah, the One' and said: 'This person has made it binding' — meaning, Paradise has become obligatory for this person.
20.iwh_id: Diriwayatkan dari Abu Umamah (radhiyallahu 'anhu) bahwa Rasulullah ﷺ melewati seorang laki-laki yang sedang membaca 'Qul Huwallahu Ahad' dan bersabda: 'Orang ini telah mewajibkannya' — yakni surga telah menjadi wajib bagi orang ini.'

21.iwh_en: It was narrated from Abu Umamah (may Allah be pleased with him) that a Bedouin came to the Prophet ﷺ and asked: 'O Prophet of Allah, how will knowledge be lifted from us while we have the Mushafs among us, and we have learned what is in them and taught them to our women, children, and servants?' The Prophet ﷺ raised his head and his face had turned red with anger. He said: 'May your mother grieve for you! These Jews and Christians have the Mushafs among them, yet they did not hold on to a single letter from what their prophets brought them. Know that among the ways knowledge departs is that those who carry it depart.' He said this three times.
21.iwh_id: Diriwayatkan dari Abu Umamah (radhiyallahu 'anhu) bahwa seorang Badui datang kepada Nabi ﷺ dan bertanya: 'Wahai Nabi Allah, bagaimana ilmu bisa terangkat dari kita sementara kita memiliki Mushaf di tengah-tengah kita, dan kita telah mempelajari apa yang ada di dalamnya serta mengajarkannya kepada wanita, anak-anak, dan pembantu kita?' Nabi ﷺ mengangkat kepalanya dan wajahnya memerah karena marah. Beliau bersabda: 'Semoga ibumu menangisimu! Orang-orang Yahudi dan Nasrani ini memiliki Mushaf di tengah-tengah mereka, namun mereka tidak berpegang pada satu huruf pun dari apa yang dibawa oleh para nabi mereka. Ketahuilah bahwa di antara cara ilmu itu pergi adalah perginya para pembawanya.' Beliau mengucapkannya tiga kali.'

22.iwh_en: It was narrated from Abu Umamah (may Allah be pleased with him) that a man said to the Prophet ﷺ: 'I passed by a cave in which there is enough water and vegetation to sustain me, and my soul tempted me to stay there and withdraw from the world.' The Prophet ﷺ said: 'I was not sent with Judaism nor with Christianity, but I was sent with the easy, upright way. By the One in Whose Hand is the soul of Muhammad, one morning or evening in the path of Allah is better than the world and everything in it. And the station of one of you in the battle line is better than sixty years of prayer.'
22.iwh_id: Diriwayatkan dari Abu Umamah (radhiyallahu 'anhu) bahwa seorang laki-laki berkata kepada Nabi ﷺ: 'Aku melewati sebuah gua yang di dalamnya terdapat air dan tumbuhan yang cukup untuk menopang hidupku, dan jiwaku tergoda untuk tinggal di sana dan mengasingkan diri dari dunia.' Nabi ﷺ bersabda: 'Aku tidak diutus dengan Yahudisme maupun Nasranisme, tetapi aku diutus dengan cara yang mudah dan lurus. Demi Dzat yang jiwa Muhammad berada di tangan-Nya, satu pagi atau petang di jalan Allah lebih baik dari dunia dan segala isinya. Dan berdirinya salah seorang dari kalian dalam barisan perang lebih baik dari enam puluh tahun shalat.'

23.iwh_en: It was narrated from Abu Umamah (may Allah be pleased with him) that the Messenger of Allah ﷺ said regarding two graves: 'These two are being punished now and are being tried in their graves.' They asked: 'For what, O Messenger of Allah?' He said: 'As for one of them, he did not protect himself from urine. As for the other, he used to spread gossip.' He then took a fresh palm branch and split it, placing it on the two graves. They asked: 'O Prophet of Allah, why did you do that?' He said: 'So that their punishment may be lightened.' They asked: 'And for how long will Allah punish them?' He said: 'That is hidden knowledge which only Allah knows.' He said: 'Were it not for your hearts becoming distressed or for your adding to the narration, you would have heard what I hear.'
23.iwh_id: Diriwayatkan dari Abu Umamah (radhiyallahu 'anhu) bahwa Rasulullah ﷺ bersabda tentang dua kubur: 'Keduanya sedang disiksa sekarang dan sedang diuji dalam kubur mereka.' Mereka bertanya: 'Karena apa, wahai Rasulullah?' Beliau bersabda: 'Adapun salah satunya, ia tidak menjaga dirinya dari air kencing. Adapun yang lainnya, ia biasa menyebarkan adu domba.' Kemudian beliau mengambil dahan kurma yang masih segar dan membelahnya, lalu meletakkannya di atas dua kubur itu. Mereka bertanya: 'Wahai Nabi Allah, mengapa engkau melakukan itu?' Beliau bersabda: 'Agar siksaan keduanya diringankan.' Mereka bertanya: 'Dan sampai kapan Allah akan menyiksa mereka?' Beliau bersabda: 'Itu adalah ilmu gaib yang hanya Allah yang mengetahuinya.' Beliau bersabda: 'Seandainya bukan karena hati kalian yang akan tertekan atau karena kalian menambah-nambah dalam narasi, niscaya kalian akan mendengar apa yang aku dengar.'

24.iwh_en: It was narrated from Abu Umamah (may Allah be pleased with him) that Sa'd ibn Abi Waqqas wept a great deal at his illness and said: 'Would that I had died.' The Prophet ﷺ said: 'O Sa'd, do you wish for death in my presence?' He repeated this three times, then said: 'O Sa'd, if you were created for Paradise, then however long your life is and however good your deeds are, that is better for you.'
24.iwh_id: Diriwayatkan dari Abu Umamah (radhiyallahu 'anhu) bahwa Sa'd ibn Abi Waqqas menangis banyak saat sakitnya dan berkata: 'Duhai, seandainya aku meninggal.' Nabi ﷺ bersabda: 'Wahai Sa'd, apakah kamu berangan-angan mati di hadapanku?' Beliau mengulanginya tiga kali, kemudian bersabda: 'Wahai Sa'd, jika kamu diciptakan untuk surga, maka semakin panjang umurmu atau semakin baik amalmu, itu lebih baik bagimu.'

25.iwh_en: It was narrated from Abu Umamah (may Allah be pleased with him) that the Messenger of Allah ﷺ said: 'Whoever claims to belong to someone other than his father, or aligns himself with other than his patrons, upon him is the continuous curse of Allah until the Day of Resurrection. A woman must not spend anything from her husband's house except with his permission.' Someone said: 'O Messenger of Allah, not even food?' He said: 'That is the best of our wealth.' Then the Messenger of Allah ﷺ said: 'A borrowed item must be returned, a gift loan is to be repaid, a debt is to be settled, and a guarantor is liable.'
25.iwh_id: Diriwayatkan dari Abu Umamah (radhiyallahu 'anhu) bahwa Rasulullah ﷺ bersabda: 'Barangsiapa mengklaim nasab kepada selain ayahnya, atau berpihak kepada selain walinya, maka atasnya adalah laknat Allah yang terus-menerus hingga Hari Kiamat. Seorang wanita tidak boleh menafkahkan sesuatu pun dari rumah suaminya kecuali dengan izinnya.' Seseorang berkata: 'Wahai Rasulullah, tidak pula makanan?' Beliau bersabda: 'Itulah harta kita yang paling utama.' Kemudian Rasulullah ﷺ bersabda: 'Barang pinjaman harus dikembalikan, pemberian sementara harus dibayar kembali, hutang harus dilunasi, dan penjamin bertanggung jawab.'

26.iwh_en: It was narrated from Abu Umamah al-Bahili (may Allah be pleased with him) that he said: 'Barley bread was never left over from the household of the Messenger of Allah ﷺ.'
26.iwh_id: Diriwayatkan dari Abu Umamah al-Bahili (radhiyallahu 'anhu) bahwa ia berkata: 'Roti gandum tidak pernah tersisa dari rumah tangga Rasulullah ﷺ.'

27.iwh_en: It was narrated from Abu Umamah (may Allah be pleased with him) that the Messenger of Allah ﷺ said: 'Through the intercession of a single man who is not a prophet, people will enter Paradise equivalent to the size of the two tribes — or one of the two tribes — of Rabi'ah and Mudar.' Someone said: 'Rabi'ah is from Mudar.' He said: 'I only say what I say.'
27.iwh_id: Diriwayatkan dari Abu Umamah (radhiyallahu 'anhu) bahwa Rasulullah ﷺ bersabda: 'Melalui syafaat seorang laki-laki yang bukan nabi, orang-orang akan masuk surga setara dengan jumlah dua kabilah — atau salah satu dari dua kabilah — Rabi'ah dan Mudhar.' Seseorang berkata: 'Rabi'ah termasuk dari Mudhar.' Beliau bersabda: 'Aku hanya mengatakan apa yang aku katakan.'

28.iwh_en: It was narrated from Abu Umamah (may Allah be pleased with him) that he said: 'I heard the Messenger of Allah ﷺ repeatedly enjoining good treatment of neighbors to the point that I thought he was going to make them heirs.'
28.iwh_id: Diriwayatkan dari Abu Umamah (radhiyallahu 'anhu) bahwa ia berkata: 'Aku mendengar Rasulullah ﷺ berulang kali mewasiatkan berbuat baik kepada tetangga hingga aku mengira beliau akan menjadikan mereka sebagai ahli waris.'

29.iwh_en: It was narrated from Abu Umamah al-Bahili (may Allah be pleased with him) that he said: 'The Messenger of Allah ﷺ took me by the hand and said to me: O Abu Umamah, among the believers are those whose hearts are softened toward me.'
29.iwh_id: Diriwayatkan dari Abu Umamah al-Bahili (radhiyallahu 'anhu) bahwa ia berkata: 'Rasulullah ﷺ memegang tanganku dan berkata kepadaku: Wahai Abu Umamah, di antara orang-orang mukmin ada yang hatinya melunak kepadaku.'

30.iwh_en: It was narrated from Abu Umamah (may Allah be pleased with him) that the Prophet ﷺ said: 'There is no man who is put in charge of ten or more people except that he will come to Allah on the Day of Resurrection with his hand chained to his neck. His righteousness will free him or his sin will destroy him. The first of it is blame, the middle of it is regret, and the last of it is disgrace on the Day of Resurrection.'
30.iwh_id: Diriwayatkan dari Abu Umamah (radhiyallahu 'anhu) bahwa Nabi ﷺ bersabda: 'Tidak ada seorang laki-laki pun yang diberi tanggung jawab atas sepuluh orang atau lebih kecuali ia akan datang kepada Allah pada Hari Kiamat dengan tangannya dirantai ke lehernya. Kebajikannya akan membebaskannya atau dosanya akan membinasakannya. Awalnya adalah celaan, tengahnya adalah penyesalan, dan akhirnya adalah kehinaan pada Hari Kiamat.'

31.iwh_en: It was narrated from Abu Umamah (may Allah be pleased with him) that he stood and said: 'I am not standing in this place of mine as a speaker. The Prophet ﷺ, when he finished eating food, would say: All praise is due to Allah — much praise, pure and blessed — and we are not sufficient for it, nor are we independent of it.'
31.iwh_id: Diriwayatkan dari Abu Umamah (radhiyallahu 'anhu) bahwa ia berdiri dan berkata: 'Aku tidak berdiri di tempat ini sebagai seorang pembicara. Nabi ﷺ, apabila selesai makan, biasa mengucapkan: Segala puji bagi Allah — pujian yang banyak, murni, dan diberkahi — dan kami tidak mampu mencukupi pujian itu, dan kami tidak bisa berdiri sendiri tanpanya.'

32.iwh_en: It was narrated from Abu Umamah (may Allah be pleased with him) that the Messenger of Allah ﷺ said: 'Whoever hopes for the days of Allah must not enjoy silk.' Khalid said to him: 'O Abu Umamah, did you hear this from the Messenger of Allah ﷺ yourself?' He said: 'O Allah, forgive me — you heard this from the Messenger of Allah ﷺ yourself. Rather, we were among a people who did not lie to us and were not accused of lying.'
32.iwh_id: Diriwayatkan dari Abu Umamah (radhiyallahu 'anhu) bahwa Rasulullah ﷺ bersabda: 'Barangsiapa mengharapkan hari-hari Allah, hendaknya ia tidak menikmati sutra.' Khalid berkata kepadanya: 'Wahai Abu Umamah, apakah kamu sendiri mendengar ini dari Rasulullah ﷺ?' Ia berkata: 'Ya Allah, ampunilah aku — kamu sendiri yang mendengar ini dari Rasulullah ﷺ. Bahkan, kami berada di antara suatu kaum yang tidak berbohong kepada kami dan tidak dituduh berdusta.'

33.iwh_en: It was narrated from Abu Umamah (may Allah be pleased with him) that the Prophet ﷺ said: 'My Lord, Mighty and Majestic, has promised me that He will admit into Paradise from my nation seventy thousand without account or punishment, and with every thousand there are seventy thousand, and three handfuls from the handfuls of my Lord, Mighty and Majestic.'
33.iwh_id: Diriwayatkan dari Abu Umamah (radhiyallahu 'anhu) bahwa Nabi ﷺ bersabda: 'Rabbku Yang Maha Perkasa lagi Maha Agung telah berjanji kepadaku bahwa Dia akan memasukkan ke surga dari umatku tujuh puluh ribu tanpa hisab dan tanpa azab, dan bersama setiap seribu ada tujuh puluh ribu, dan tiga genggaman dari genggaman Rabbku Yang Maha Perkasa lagi Maha Agung.'

34.iwh_en: It was narrated from Abu Umamah (may Allah be pleased with him) that the Messenger of Allah ﷺ said: 'Whoever walks to pray the Duha prayer will have the reward of a pilgrim in the state of ihram. Whoever walks to pray the Duha voluntary prayer will have the reward of one performing 'Umrah. A prayer performed right after another prayer with no idle talk between them is written in Illiyyun.' Abu Umamah also said: 'Going out in the morning and evening to these mosques is from jihad in the path of Allah.'
34.iwh_id: Diriwayatkan dari Abu Umamah (radhiyallahu 'anhu) bahwa Rasulullah ﷺ bersabda: 'Barangsiapa berjalan untuk melaksanakan shalat Dhuha, maka baginya seperti pahala seorang yang berhaji dalam keadaan ihram. Barangsiapa berjalan untuk melaksanakan shalat sunnah Dhuha, maka baginya seperti pahala orang yang berumrah. Shalat yang dikerjakan tepat setelah shalat lainnya tanpa diselingi perkataan sia-sia di antara keduanya, dicatat di Illiyyun.' Abu Umamah juga berkata: 'Keluar di pagi dan sore hari ke masjid-masjid ini termasuk jihad di jalan Allah.'

35.iwh_en: It was narrated from Abu Umamah (may Allah be pleased with him), on the authority of someone who had seen the Messenger of Allah ﷺ, that the Messenger of Allah ﷺ went toward Mina on the Day of Tarwiyah, and beside him was Bilal with a stick in his hand covered with a cloth that he used to shade the Messenger of Allah ﷺ.
35.iwh_id: Diriwayatkan dari Abu Umamah (radhiyallahu 'anhu), dari seseorang yang pernah melihat Rasulullah ﷺ, bahwa Rasulullah ﷺ berangkat menuju Mina pada Hari Tarwiyah, dan di sisinya ada Bilal yang memegang tongkat yang ditutupi kain yang digunakannya untuk menaungi Rasulullah ﷺ.'

36.iwh_en: It was narrated from Abu Umamah (may Allah be pleased with him) that the Messenger of Allah ﷺ said: 'No servant has been permitted anything better than two rak'ahs he prays. And goodness is scattered over the head of the servant as long as he is in his prayer. The servants have not drawn near to Allah with anything like what came from Him' — meaning the Quran.
36.iwh_id: Diriwayatkan dari Abu Umamah (radhiyallahu 'anhu) bahwa Rasulullah ﷺ bersabda: 'Tidak ada seorang hamba pun yang diizinkan sesuatu yang lebih baik dari dua rakaat yang ia shalat. Dan kebaikan tersebar di atas kepala seorang hamba selama ia dalam shalatnya. Para hamba tidak pernah mendekat kepada Allah dengan sesuatu yang semisal apa yang keluar dari-Nya' — yakni Al-Qur'an.'

37.iwh_en: It was narrated from Abu Umamah (may Allah be pleased with him) that the Messenger of Allah ﷺ said: 'Indeed Allah sent me as a mercy to the worlds and as guidance to the worlds. And my Lord, Mighty and Majestic, commanded me to destroy musical instruments, flutes, idols, crosses, and the affairs of pre-Islamic ignorance. My Lord, Mighty and Majestic, has sworn by His Might that no servant of Mine drinks a sip of wine except that I will give him the same amount of festering water on the Day of Resurrection — whether he is forgiven or punished. And whoever gives it to drink to a young, weak Muslim child, I will give him the same amount of festering water on the Day of Resurrection — whether forgiven or punished. And whoever abandons it out of fear of Me, I will give him to drink from the pure cisterns on the Day of Resurrection.' He said this about female musicians.
37.iwh_id: Diriwayatkan dari Abu Umamah (radhiyallahu 'anhu) bahwa Rasulullah ﷺ bersabda: 'Sesungguhnya Allah mengutus aku sebagai rahmat bagi semesta alam dan sebagai petunjuk bagi semesta alam. Dan Rabbku Yang Maha Perkasa lagi Maha Agung memerintahkanku untuk menghancurkan alat musik, seruling, berhala, salib, dan urusan-urusan jahiliah. Rabbku Yang Maha Perkasa lagi Maha Agung telah bersumpah demi keagungan-Nya bahwa tidak ada seorang hamba-Ku yang meminum seteguk khamar kecuali Aku akan memberikannya minuman nanah yang sama pada Hari Kiamat — baik ia diampuni maupun disiksa. Dan barangsiapa meminumkannya kepada seorang anak kecil yang lemah dan muslim, Aku akan memberikannya minuman nanah yang sama pada Hari Kiamat — baik diampuni maupun disiksa. Dan barangsiapa meninggalkannya karena takut kepada-Ku, Aku akan meminumkannya dari telaga-telaga suci pada Hari Kiamat.' Beliau mengucapkan ini tentang para penyanyi wanita.'

38.iwh_en: It was narrated from Abu Umamah (may Allah be pleased with him), attributed to the Prophet ﷺ, that he said: 'The Beast will emerge and brand people on their snouts. Then they will intermingle with you until a man buys a camel and is asked: From whom did you buy it? And he replies: I bought it from one of those branded.'
38.iwh_id: Diriwayatkan dari Abu Umamah (radhiyallahu 'anhu), yang disandarkan kepada Nabi ﷺ, bahwa beliau bersabda: 'Binatang Melata akan keluar dan menandai orang-orang di ujung hidung mereka. Kemudian mereka bercampur baur di antara kalian hingga seseorang membeli seekor unta dan ditanya: Dari siapa kamu membelinya? Dan ia menjawab: Aku membelinya dari salah seorang yang sudah ditandai.'

39.iwh_en: It was narrated from Abu Umamah (may Allah be pleased with him) that the Messenger of Allah ﷺ said: 'A visitor of the sick wades through mercy.' The Messenger of Allah ﷺ placed his hand on his thigh then said: 'Like this — going toward and away. And when he sits with him, mercy envelops him.'
39.iwh_id: Diriwayatkan dari Abu Umamah (radhiyallahu 'anhu) bahwa Rasulullah ﷺ bersabda: 'Seorang pengunjung orang sakit berjalan melalui rahmat.' Rasulullah ﷺ meletakkan tangannya di pahanya kemudian bersabda: 'Seperti ini — datang dan pergi. Dan ketika ia duduk di sisinya, rahmat menyelimutinya.'

40.iwh_en: It was narrated from Abu Umamah (may Allah be pleased with him) that the Prophet ﷺ performed wudu, rinsing his mouth three times, inhaling water three times, and washing his face. He would wipe the two corners of his eyes. He said: 'The Prophet ﷺ would wipe his head once. And he used to say: The ears are part of the head.'
40.iwh_id: Diriwayatkan dari Abu Umamah (radhiyallahu 'anhu) bahwa Nabi ﷺ berwudu, berkumur-kumur tiga kali, menghirup air tiga kali, dan membasuh wajahnya. Beliau mengusap dua sudut matanya. Ia berkata: 'Nabi ﷺ biasa mengusap kepalanya sekali. Dan beliau biasa bersabda: Kedua telinga adalah bagian dari kepala.'

41.iwh_en: It was narrated from Abu Umamah (may Allah be pleased with him) that a woman came to the Messenger of Allah ﷺ with two sons and while she was pregnant. Whatever she asked of him that day, he gave her. Then he said: 'Pregnant women, mothers, and the compassionate — were it not for what they do to their husbands, they would have entered Paradise.'
41.iwh_id: Diriwayatkan dari Abu Umamah (radhiyallahu 'anhu) bahwa seorang wanita datang kepada Rasulullah ﷺ bersama dua anaknya dalam keadaan hamil. Apapun yang ia minta dari beliau hari itu, beliau memberikannya. Kemudian beliau bersabda: 'Para wanita yang hamil, melahirkan, dan penyayang — seandainya bukan karena apa yang mereka lakukan kepada suami-suami mereka, niscaya mereka akan masuk surga.'

42.iwh_en: It was narrated from Abu Umamah al-Bahili (may Allah be pleased with him) that the Prophet ﷺ said: 'Modesty and restraint in speech are two branches of faith. Obscenity and eloquence used for evil are two branches of hypocrisy.'
42.iwh_id: Diriwayatkan dari Abu Umamah al-Bahili (radhiyallahu 'anhu) bahwa Nabi ﷺ bersabda: 'Rasa malu dan sedikit bicara adalah dua cabang keimanan. Kekejian mulut dan kefasihan yang digunakan untuk keburukan adalah dua cabang kemunafikan.'

43.iwh_en: It was narrated from Abu Ghalib, from Abu Umamah (may Allah be pleased with him), that he said: 'The Messenger of Allah ﷺ used to perform witr with nine rak'ahs. When he grew older and heavier, he performed witr with seven rak'ahs, then prayed two rak'ahs while seated, reciting in them: When the Earth is shaken and Say: O you disbelievers.'
43.iwh_id: Diriwayatkan dari Abu Ghalib, dari Abu Umamah (radhiyallahu 'anhu), bahwa ia berkata: 'Rasulullah ﷺ biasa melaksanakan shalat witir dengan sembilan rakaat. Ketika beliau bertambah tua dan gemuk, beliau melaksanakan witir dengan tujuh rakaat, kemudian shalat dua rakaat dalam keadaan duduk, membaca di dalamnya: Apabila bumi diguncangkan dan Katakanlah: Wahai orang-orang yang kafir.'

44.iwh_en: It was narrated that Abu Umamah al-Bahili (may Allah be pleased with him) entered Damascus and saw the heads of the Haruriyyah (Khawarij) mounted. He said: 'Dogs of the Fire! Dogs of the Fire! Dogs of the Fire! The worst of those killed under the sky, while the best of those killed are those whom they killed.' Then he wept. A man stood and said to him: 'O Abu Umamah, is what you say from your own opinion or something you heard?' He said: 'I would then be very bold indeed — how could I say this from my own opinion?' He said: 'I heard it more than once or twice.' He was asked: 'What makes you weep?' He said: 'I weep for their departure from Islam — these who divided and took their religion as sects.'
44.iwh_id: Diriwayatkan bahwa Abu Umamah al-Bahili (radhiyallahu 'anhu) masuk ke Damaskus dan melihat kepala-kepala Haruriyyah (Khawarij) yang dipancang. Ia berkata: 'Anjing-anjing neraka! Anjing-anjing neraka! Anjing-anjing neraka! Seburuk-buruk orang yang terbunuh di bawah naungan langit, sementara sebaik-baik orang yang terbunuh adalah orang-orang yang mereka bunuh.' Kemudian ia menangis. Seorang laki-laki berdiri dan berkata kepadanya: 'Wahai Abu Umamah, apakah yang kamu katakan ini berasal dari pendapatmu sendiri atau sesuatu yang kamu dengar?' Ia berkata: 'Aku sungguh berani jika demikian — bagaimana mungkin aku mengatakan ini dari pendapatku sendiri?' Ia berkata: 'Aku mendengarnya lebih dari sekali atau dua kali.' Ia ditanya: 'Apa yang membuatmu menangis?' Ia berkata: 'Aku menangis karena keluarnya mereka dari Islam — orang-orang yang berpecah-belah dan menjadikan agama mereka sebagai sekte-sekte.'

45.iwh_en: It was narrated from Abu Umamah (may Allah be pleased with him) that the Prophet ﷺ said: 'These two are a congregation' — referring to praying in a group.
45.iwh_id: Diriwayatkan dari Abu Umamah (radhiyallahu 'anhu) bahwa Nabi ﷺ bersabda: 'Keduanya ini adalah jemaah' — mengacu pada shalat berjamaah.'

46.iwh_en: It was narrated from Abu Umamah (may Allah be pleased with him) that the Prophet ﷺ said: 'Whoever initiates the greeting of peace is closer to Allah, the Mighty and Majestic, and to His Messenger ﷺ.'
46.iwh_id: Diriwayatkan dari Abu Umamah (radhiyallahu 'anhu) bahwa Nabi ﷺ bersabda: 'Barangsiapa memulai salam, maka dialah yang lebih dekat kepada Allah Yang Maha Perkasa lagi Maha Agung dan kepada Rasul-Nya ﷺ.'

47.iwh_en: It was narrated from Abu Umamah (may Allah be pleased with him) that the Messenger of Allah ﷺ said: 'Three things follow the deceased: his family, his wealth, and his deeds — two return and one stays. His family and wealth return, while his deeds stay. When a man dies, his deeds are cut off except for three: a continuing charity whose reward continues upon him as long as it runs; a righteous child who supplicates for him; and knowledge by which others benefit.'
47.iwh_id: Diriwayatkan dari Abu Umamah (radhiyallahu 'anhu) bahwa Rasulullah ﷺ bersabda: 'Tiga hal mengikuti orang yang meninggal: keluarganya, hartanya, dan amalnya — dua kembali dan satu tinggal. Keluarga dan hartanya kembali, sementara amalnya tinggal. Apabila seseorang meninggal, amalnya terputus kecuali tiga: sedekah yang terus mengalir yang pahalanya terus mengalir atasnya selama sedekah itu berjalan; anak shalih yang mendoakannya; dan ilmu yang bermanfaat bagi orang lain.'

48.iwh_en: It was narrated from Abu Umamah (may Allah be pleased with him) that the Messenger of Allah ﷺ said: 'A group from my nation will remain upon the truth, victorious over their enemies, overwhelming them. Those who oppose them will not harm them, except for the hardship that strikes them, until the command of Allah comes to them while they are in that state.' They asked: 'O Messenger of Allah, where are they?' He said: 'In Jerusalem and the surroundings of Jerusalem.'
48.iwh_id: Diriwayatkan dari Abu Umamah (radhiyallahu 'anhu) bahwa Rasulullah ﷺ bersabda: 'Sekelompok dari umatku akan tetap berada di atas kebenaran, menang atas musuh-musuh mereka, menguasai mereka. Orang-orang yang menentang mereka tidak akan membahayakan mereka, kecuali kesulitan yang menimpa mereka, hingga datang perintah Allah kepada mereka sementara mereka dalam keadaan demikian.' Mereka bertanya: 'Wahai Rasulullah, di manakah mereka?' Beliau bersabda: 'Di Baitul Maqdis dan sekitar Baitul Maqdis.'

49.iwh_en: It was narrated from Abu Umamah (may Allah be pleased with him) that a man asked the Messenger of Allah ﷺ: 'Which charity is best?' He ﷺ said: 'The shade of a tent in the path of Allah, or the service of a servant in the path of Allah, or providing a mount in the path of Allah.'
49.iwh_id: Diriwayatkan dari Abu Umamah (radhiyallahu 'anhu) bahwa seorang laki-laki bertanya kepada Rasulullah ﷺ: 'Sedekah manakah yang paling utama?' Beliau ﷺ bersabda: 'Naungan kemah di jalan Allah, atau pelayanan seorang pelayan di jalan Allah, atau menyediakan tunggangan di jalan Allah.'

50.iwh_en: It was narrated from Abu Umamah (may Allah be pleased with him) that the Messenger of Allah ﷺ said: 'Whoever takes the right of a Muslim through an oath — Allah has made the Fire obligatory for him and forbidden Paradise to him.' A man said to him: 'Even if it is something insignificant, O Messenger of Allah?' He ﷺ said: 'Even if it is a twig of the arak tree.'
50.iwh_id: Diriwayatkan dari Abu Umamah (radhiyallahu 'anhu) bahwa Rasulullah ﷺ bersabda: 'Barangsiapa mengambil hak seorang muslim dengan sumpahnya — Allah mewajibkan neraka baginya dan mengharamkan surga atasnya.' Seorang laki-laki berkata kepadanya: 'Meskipun itu sesuatu yang sepele, wahai Rasulullah?' Beliau ﷺ bersabda: 'Meskipun itu hanya sebatang ranting pohon arak.'
